title: Soul rant
wikitext:
[]: Soul rant             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Wed Aug 02, 2006 2:41 pm So I've been thinking in the shower (where else?) about the while soul thing in NGE. At first, I thought of what happans in Third Impact-ATF crashes, soul flies off, body tangifies. I figured that without the ATF the soul can just go wherever (It was drawn to Lilith in this case) and without a soul the body goes splat. Then I remembered the dead NERV dudes got tangified too- hence, they still had an ATF that crumbled, or at least a soul that was collected. This made me wonder about dead people in EVA-soul-mechanics; do souls remain in the dead bodies? And if so, what happans to a soul when the body decays and vanishes? Rei does mention in her monolouge in ep.14 that 'Humans came from the Red Soil'. Red is Rei's hated color, and the color of souls. The Earth becomes red after 3I, and the Sea of LCL turns red after the Egg goes boom. That made me think of Rei literally meant the Red Earth, as in, the ground being full of souls and such. They wouldn't be physically visible, but meh.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 02, 2006 3:06 pm Souls appear to hang around the corpse for a while at least - hence the retireval of Ketucky Fried ReiII.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Wed Aug 02, 2006 3:13 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Souls appear to hang around the corpse for a while at least - hence the retireval of Ketucky Fried ReiII.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Thu Aug 03, 2006 7:47 am The first thing that came to my mind about the Red Soil is war. War=people kill people=ground get messy/gorey/bloody=earth gets red. And also, the core of Earth is also red. And could also be called soil in a certain way of looking at it... :S

[]: Re: Soul rant             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 05, 2006 11:02 pm Legend of the Past wrote: At first, I thought of what happans in Third Impact-ATF crashes, soul flies off, body tangifies.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Aug 05, 2006 11:07 pm [img]Tangification happens first -- check out Keel. After what little flesh on him turns into LCL, a red dot -- i.e., his soul -- floats up. Strangely, I don't recall seeing an intense ATF blur on him before he went splat, whereas this is quite prominent with Hyuga, Aoba, and Fuyutsuki when they are made to succumb to the Touch of Death.[/img] What blur? They just went splat. Keel didn't splatter because....there wasn't enough biomass left to go splat. I just sorta melted off his machine parts and went thud to the ground.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Aug 06, 2006 2:28 am SEELE 08 wrote: What blur? They just went splat. Keel didn't splatter because....there wasn't enough biomass left to go splat. I just sorta melted off his machine parts and went thud to the ground.

[]: Re: Soul rant             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Aug 06, 2006 2:35 am Reichu wrote: Tangification happens first -- check out Keel. After what little flesh on him turns into LCL, a red dot -- i.e., his soul -- floats up. Strangely, I don't recall seeing an intense ATF blur on him before he went splat, whereas this is quite prominent with Hyuga, Aoba, and Fuyutsuki when they are made to succumb to the Touch of Death. (Maya bursts into LCL offscreen.) Apparently an incarnation of Rei (= Lilith) goes to you, kills you, and brings your soul back to the Mothership.

[]: Re: Soul rant             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 06, 2006 8:12 am Legend of the Past wrote: While I'll take your word for it, Rachel, I can't recall it happaning either.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Aug 06, 2006 11:19 am Maybe you just can't see it in the Mangle version:

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Aug 06, 2006 12:15 pm I recall that a lot of red lil' thingees sprouted out from the Earth in EOE. Perhaps watching it again might help. It was like a rain of blood falling up, as if the gravity would've been canceled. And big shiny crosses also appear as I remember that scene. Why do people think that the soul has a specific location in the body? It is just in your body. And just who said that the soul is 3 dimensional? If it would have anything to do with 3D matter, than we would have something in our body which would be similar to the core the Angels have. But perhaps the NGE universe has a different way of seeing things. So I'll just sit and see what will happen. Oh, by the way: what is a solar plexus? I've heared it before somewhere but I still don't know what it is. If there would be an "n" instead of "pl" then everything would be quite clear.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 06, 2006 12:38 pm http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_plexus

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Aug 06, 2006 3:04 pm SEELE 08 wrote: Maybe you just can't see it in the Mangle version:

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Aug 06, 2006 3:55 pm The entire scene looks blurry. Not just the character. I'd chalk it up to the production quality.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Aug 06, 2006 7:54 pm You know, it takes an awesome degree of obstinancy to see the same effect clearly demonstrated in all three cases, immediately before the characters go splat, and still pretend that nothing intentional is actually going on here. Do you realize how infinitesimally unlikely it is that the lame hypothesis ("production quality" :roll: ), with which you attempt to explain away an effect that the rest of us can all plainly see, is actually correct? The 'AT Field blur' is a well established visual convention that appears throughout the series:

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Aug 06, 2006 8:42 pm Shin, do you want to fight because I don't, so tone it down buddy. I seriously don't see what you guys are seeing, and not everyone claims to see it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Aug 06, 2006 9:37 pm I believe the technical term for the effect here is "soft focus". If my thousands of collective video media consumption hours have taught me correctly, the technique is often used in two circumstances. One, when we are viewing a dreamscape or distant memory. And two, when we are viewing parasides, angels, or similarly sublime scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Aug 06, 2006 9:46 pm parasides

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 07, 2006 12:03 am Shin-seiki, those examples are way too small to actually see the ATF Blurs. Animations are probably the best way to show the effect off. Some random other instances: - EVA-01 deploying ATF (#03) - EVA-01 regenerating arm (#19) - EVA-01 throwing ATF at Zeruel (#19) - Armisael breaking through Zero's ATF (#23) - Angel Tower sprouts from Zero (#23') - Armisael morphing into Rei torso (#23') - Kaworu deploying ATF at EVA-02's cage (#24) - Rei deploying ATF during Eva fight (#24) - Spear of Longinus pulling out of Moon (#26') - Eva Series' heads morphing (#26') And Seele, sorry, but this visual convention shows up all over the series in association with ATFs -- again, and again, and again, and again... This is something you really don't have to be dense about, unless that is your goal.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Mon Aug 07, 2006 12:25 am So just because I don't agree with a few people who are seeing blurring effects I'm not....I'm dense. Nice personal attack. If it bother you all so much that I don't agree with you on a minor issue you must have serious issues. But on to the actually arguement, not the one about my intelligence that you all seem so fixated on. If you want my SAT and ACT scores I can give them to you, I'm sorry I didn't record my SAT II scores for posterity, so you'll have to do without. All I see when an ATF goes up is a force field like thing, the only blurring is the ATF between the eva and an angel or whatever. With the people, I don't see squat.
